title: Message Bar
description: Message Bar keeps important inline status visible in layout. Use it for confirmations, warnings, blockers, and contextual guidance that should remain present until the user acts or the state changes.
intro: Message Bar renders persistent inline feedback with semantic variants, multiple appearances, optional actions, dismiss support, and richer projected content. It fits places where a toast would disappear too quickly and a dialog would be too heavy.

featureSections.title: Basic inline notice
featureSections.description: Use a short title and message when users need persistent context inside a page, panel, or form rather than a temporary notification.

featureSections.title: Semantic variants
featureSections.description: Choose variant by meaning, not decoration: success for completion, warning for caution, danger for blockers, and info or primary for guidance and active notices.

featureSections.title: Appearances
featureSections.description: Tint is a balanced default, filled is stronger when the message must dominate, outline works well on busy surfaces, and subtle or transparent fit calmer embedded layouts.

featureSections.title: Single-line and multiline layout
featureSections.description: Use single-line mode for compact rows and tool surfaces. Switch to multiline when message length or actions need more space and you do not want cramped wrapping.

featureSections.title: Built-in actions and dismiss
featureSections.description: Quick actions work well for recovery, review, or follow-up flows. Pair them with dismiss only when users can safely postpone the message.

featureSections.title: Rich content and custom action area
featureSections.description: Projected content lets you add secondary details, summaries, or compact inline metrics. The custom actions slot is useful when simple quick actions are not enough.

accessibility.title: Role and live region
accessibility.body:
`MessageBarComponent` uses `role="alert"` with assertive live announcements for the `danger` variant, and `role="status"` with polite announcements for other variants.

| Variant group | Live region |
| --- | --- |
| `danger` | `role="alert"` + assertive announcement |
| all other variants | `role="status"` + polite announcement |

accessibility.title: Atomic announcement and actions
accessibility.body:
The root sets `aria-atomic="true"`, so assistive technologies announce the message as one unit instead of piecemeal fragments.

| Element | Keyboard behavior |
| --- | --- |
| dismiss button | standard button keyboard support |
| quick actions | standard button keyboard support |
| custom `slot="actions"` content | follows the projected control semantics |

accessibility.title: Dismiss control
accessibility.body: When `dismissible` is true, the dismiss control receives an accessible label from the shared i18n message. Keep visible copy concise so the main announcement remains understandable before users reach the dismiss button.

apiSections.title: MessageBarComponent - ui-message-bar
apiSections.lead: Standalone inline status surface for persistent contextual feedback. It supports semantic styling, optional quick actions, dismiss, and projected content without forcing a separate overlay or toast queue.

blocks.title: Inputs
rows: Optional heading for the message. Use it when users need a fast summary before reading the detail text.
Primary supporting text for the current state, warning, or recommendation.
Quick actions rendered as buttons in the aside area. Good for compact follow-up actions such as review, retry, or learn more.
Optional explicit icon override. When omitted, the icon is derived from the variant.
Semantic intent of the message: guidance, success, warning, or danger.
Visual weight of the surface. Use stronger appearances only when the notice should compete harder with page content.
Spacing and typography density for compact rows versus more prominent panel notices.
Shows or hides the leading status icon.
Adds the dismiss control when users may hide the notice.
Controls compact single-line versus expanded multiline layout.

blocks.title: Outputs
rows: Emitted when the built-in dismiss control is activated.
Emitted after a quick action runs so parent code can react, log analytics, or update local state.

paragraphs: Default projection renders inside the main content area below the built-in title and message fields.
Projected elements marked with slot="actions" render in the aside area after quick actions, which is useful for custom layouts beyond the QuickAction[] shortcut.
blocks.title: Notes
Related mechanisms
items: QuickAction - compact action model used to render built-in action buttons.
IconComponent - renders the variant-derived or custom icon.
